structuredReasoning.inference: Purchase order is the external commitment to a supplier after internal requisition approval.
structuredReasoning.conclusion: External commitment document issued to a supplier to procure goods or services — distinct from internal requisition.

knowledgeDecision.decision: Accept purchase_order as enterprise meaning bridge for PAS-001B procurement-document-type purchase_order.

whyRejected.Collapse PO into requisition: PO is the supplier-facing commitment; requisition is internal demand.

meaning.canonical: External commitment document issued to a supplier to procure goods or services — distinct from internal requisition.
meaning.business: Purchase order / supplier commitment.
meaning.engineering: procurement-document-type purchase_order · KV-PROC · meaning only; wire in kernel

lineage.origin: PAS-001B procurement wire vocabulary · gap report §B.2.
lineage.futureDirection: Runtime procurement module via approved slice.

misconceptions.why: Requisition is internal approval to buy; PO is external supplier commitment.
misconceptions.correct: PO follows approved requisition and binds supplier terms.

premises: Procurement references trading partners via PAS-001 SupplierId without duplicating kernel parsers.
structuredReasoning.inference: Supplier is the trading partner master reference in procurement context — identity wire in kernel, meaning accepted here.
structuredReasoning.conclusion: Trading partner master reference used in procurement — SupplierId wire identity without duplicating kernel parsers.

knowledgeDecision.decision: Accept supplier (not vendor) as enterprise term aligned to PAS-001 SupplierId and gap report §B.2.

whyRejected.Use vendor atomId: Platform wire and registry use SupplierId; vendor wording is historical drift.

meaning.canonical: Trading partner master reference used in procurement — SupplierId wire identity without duplicating kernel parsers.
meaning.business: Supplier / trading partner in procurement context.

lineage.futureDirection: BMD supplier persistence via ERP-MODULES gap report §persistence (authorized slice handoff required).

misconceptions.why: Afenda wire vocabulary standardizes on SupplierId.
misconceptions.correct: Supplier atom references kernel SupplierId — not a parallel vendor parser.

premises: Procurement wire vocabulary includes rfq document type and permission domain.
structuredReasoning.inference: Procurement RFQ is the request-for-quotation sourcing document inviting supplier quotes before PO issuance.
structuredReasoning.conclusion: Request for quotation sourcing document used to solicit supplier pricing before purchase order commitment.

knowledgeDecision.decision: Accept procurement_rfq as enterprise meaning bridge for PAS-001B rfq wire vocabulary.

whyRejected.Generic sourcing atom only: RFQ is a distinct document family with governed permission domain.

meaning.canonical: Request for quotation sourcing document used to solicit supplier pricing before purchase order commitment.
meaning.business: Request for quotation (RFQ) / procurement sourcing document.

lineage.origin: PAS-001B procurement wire vocabulary · gap report §B.2.
lineage.futureDirection: Runtime procurement RFQ flows via approved slice.

misconceptions.why: PO commits to a supplier; RFQ solicits quotes before commitment.
misconceptions.correct: RFQ precedes PO in competitive sourcing paths.
